title: chromium --shm-size vs pod catch 22
body: I am running into an issue with a customer using electronjs in a container. Chromium is immediately using up all of the memory allowed for /dev/shm. Outside a pod formation I can set --shm-size=1g and get around the problem. However, the electronjs pod has to communicate on "localhost" to another pod and we can't change the code. If I try to use --shm-size in a pod, I get the error: cannot set shmsize when running in the host IPC Namespace. So, i'm in a bit of a cache 22 situation. Is there a good solution for this out of memory issue involving chromium?

comments.body: can you try `--net=slirp4netns` in conjunction with `--shm-size` on the pod level? This should allow you to still be able to communicate on localhost and set the shm.

`--shm-size` for pod create was just added last week: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14625

As workaround you can also set `--shm-size` on the container if you create the pod with `--share net,uts`
